sections.paragraph:
μεταβάλλω fut. 3 sg. and pl. μεταβαλεῖ,-οῦσιν LXX; aor. μετέβαλον LXX; pf. ptc. fem. acc. μεταβεβληκυῖαν Jdth 10:7; 2 aor. mid. μετεβαλόμην. Pass.: fut. 3 sg. μεταβληθήσεται (GrBar); aor. ptc. pl. μεταβληθέντες (OdeSol) (Hom.+; apolog. [only act. and pass.] exc. Tat., Mel.) in our lit. only mid. to change one’s way of thinking, change (Thu. et al.) abs. (Pla., Gorg. 481e μεταβαλόμενος λέγεις; X., Hell. 2, 3, 31; IMagnMai 115, 20; 4 Macc 6:18; Philo, Mos. 1, 147; Jos., Bell. 1, 296; Just., A I, 12, 11) Ac 28:6. W. εἴς τι turn to someth. (Jos., Ant. 5, 256; ἀπὸ σκότους εἰς τὸ φῶς OdeSol 11:19; TestDan 4:3) εἰς νέαν ζύμην to the new leaven (=Christ) IMg 10:2 (cp. OdeSol 11:19).—M-M. TW.

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular
  2. pl. = plural

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

fem. fem. = feminine

acc. acc. = accusative

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

OdeSol OdeSol = Gk. Ode of Solomon 11: PBodmer XI; III a.d.—List 2

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

apolog. apolog. = apologist(s)

act. act. = active

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

exc. exc. = except

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

abs. abs. = absolute

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

IMagnMai IMagnMai = Die Inschriften von Magnesia am Mäander—Lists 3

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

TestDan TestDan = Testament of Dan, s. Test12Patr—List 2

IMg IMg = Ignatius to the Magnesians—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 638
